Real Heroes eat preserved dry rations.

text: Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.

text: Sorry, there's not enough room to see that here.
speaker: narrator

text: "Even as we fought, I could detect your skills improving through practice."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "It was most prudent that you should give in to my superior skill."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Still, you seem to tire more easily than you should."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Be more aware of the angle with which your weapon approaches your opponent.  The thrust bites quick and deep, but the swing will do more damage if it connects."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The art of dancing would be a good skill for you to practice, my friend.  The balance of the body over the rapidly moving feet has won many a contest."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "You have a tendency to overbalance when you dodge a blow.  This will adversely affect the timing and effectiveness of your counterattack."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Put your attention to the control of your legs when you duck a swing.  Your springiness makes you seem to bounce, and a good opponent (like myself) will always take advantage of the opening."
speaker: weapon-master

text:
"If you would like a stretcher, my weak, tired friend...
You seem a shadow of the person who began this little lesson."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Should we meet again, I would not be adverse to another go-round.  Farewell, friend!"
speaker: weapon-master

text: You are in the courtyard of Castle Spielburg.
speaker: narrator

text: The courtyard is paved with granite paving stones, carefully placed by a skilled craftsman.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nobody else here.
speaker: narrator

text: There are two guards near the castle door.
speaker: narrator

text: He isn't here at the moment.
speaker: narrator

text: What you see, along with what you smell, gives the impression that the building on the east side of the courtyard contains the stables.
speaker: narrator

text: On the west side of the courtyard is a building that looks like soldier's barracks.
speaker: narrator

text: As a matter of fact, there's a soldier sleeping in front of it now.
speaker: narrator

text: The door to the Baron's castle is well guarded.
speaker: narrator

text: As you look back at the castle gate, you see a line of bushes along the south wall and the gatehouse and portcullis.
speaker: narrator

text: You barely get to sleep when you hear the guards searching the castle grounds for bums.  You decide it's time to make like a tree and leave.
speaker: narrator

text: There are too many guards around.
speaker: narrator

text: This tastes as sour as it smells, and it burns your throat as you swallow it. Still, it isn't the worst beer you've ever drunk.
speaker: narrator

text: You know, that actually tasted fine! This really isn't such a bad place, after all, and the bartender reminds you of an old friend you used to know.
speaker: narrator

text: Suddenly, you don't feel so good...
speaker: narrator

text: Smells like Troll's Sweat. Tastes like Troll's Sweat. By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat....
speaker: narrator

text: You've never tasted anything like it before.
speaker: narrator

text: Too much beer.
speaker: narrator

text: ...and one Troll's Sweat is too many.
speaker: narrator

text: Ohhhhhhhhh woooowwwwwww!!!
speaker: hero

text: Maybe you really shouldn't have tried the Dragon's Breath!  Better luck next time, and we hope you saved your game.
speaker: narrator

text: The late frosts of winter give way to to the greenery of spring. There is the crisp smell of cedar in the air. This forest seems to be unusually still and quiet.
speaker: narrator

text: It IS a ghost...a transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragment of undulating ectoplasm!
speaker: narrator

text: You might be near a graveyard!
speaker: narrator

text: There are no ghosts here to look at.
speaker: narrator

text: Pine needles, moss and early spring grasses cover the forest floor.
speaker: narrator

text: The low-lying bushes form tight tangles between the trees.
speaker: narrator

text: The valley floor is covered by fine, moist soil with few large rocks.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't get ghosts.  Ghosts get YOU!
speaker: narrator

text: There aren't any.
speaker: narrator

text: That isn't drinkable -- it's an oil.
speaker: narrator

text: You pry at the sealed lid of the chest, and...
speaker: narrator

text: You hack and pry at the chest, but you are not strong enough to force its lid.
speaker: narrator

text: You hear a very satisfying "Snick" as your pick catches the lock just right.  Then you sense powerful magics dissipating around the lock, and your hands shake as you realize you have narrowly avoided some sort of Magical Doom.
speaker: narrator

text: Your fingers shift slightly, and you feel an odd prickling sensation coming from the lock.  You have a bad feeling about this.
speaker: narrator

text: Mighty careless of you to walk right in front of that knife!  Or did the Chief Thief hit you on purpose?  Or did you just discover an obscure bug in the program???  Maybe you'll never really know.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't believe the Chief Thief's reflexes as he instantly reacts to your intention to pull a fast one.  A dagger flies like lightning and....
speaker: narrator

text: Trouble with sharp, pointy objects
speaker: narrator

text: These guys are MEAN!
speaker: narrator

text: You cannot escape this encounter.
speaker: narrator

text: You cannot make the arcane gestures to cast spells while carrying your shield.
speaker: narrator

text: This might not be the best time for practicing non-combat spells.
speaker: narrator

text: Go ahead!
speaker: narrator

text: You're too close.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have time for that.
speaker: narrator

text: Caught in combat with neither sword nor dagger, you are unable to resist the monster's fierce onslaught.  Try to be better equipped in your next life.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the brigand leader.  From the look in her eyes you've only got seconds to live.
speaker: narrator

text: Casting a spell isn't useful now.
speaker: narrator

text: She's not in a talking mood.
speaker: narrator

text: Not a good idea.
speaker: narrator

text: No time for that.
speaker: narrator

text: The Brigand leader looks tough!  This may be the toughest battle of your career so far.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a small sign which reads "Booby Trap".  You are now in a pitiful situation as you hear the brigands approach to see the booby they've caught.  You think to yourself, "If it looks like a trap, and it feels like a trap, then maybe it IS a trap".
say: If it looks like a trap, and it feels like a trap, then maybe it IS a trap.
speaker: hero

text: You feel disoriented.
speaker: narrator

text: How does he DO this?
speaker: narrator

text: There's an ECHO IN HERE...Echo In Here...echo in here...
speaker: narrator

text: M. C. Escher would LOVE this place.
speaker: narrator

text: OK, enough fooling around!
speaker: narrator

text: This is getting old!
speaker: narrator

text: A person COULD get used to this.
speaker: narrator

text: You carefully bend down and reach into the nest.
speaker: narrator

text: In the nest, you find a gold ring.
speaker: narrator

text: You take the ring and move carefully back along the limb.
speaker: narrator

text: Made it!  Now back down the tree.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold doesn't look very calm.  In fact, it looks totally unaffected by your spell.

text: Uh oh, you've woken the Kobold.

text: You now have the Kobold's key.  Nice work!

text: "Elsa is right next door.  I'll go set up the secret exit for your escape."

text: Get closer for a good look.
speaker: narrator

text: The remaining pages are blank, awaiting further tales of adventure.
speaker: narrator

text: The other pages record the exploits of the great adventurers of Spielburg's past.  The names Wolfgang Abenteuer and Baron Stefan von Spielburg appear most often.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like this old Guild Master doesn't do too much adventuring any more.  Still, he looks like he was plenty tough in his day, and he probably has many a tale to tell.
speaker: narrator

text: The fire blazing cheerily in the fireplace helps warm the Guild Master's old bones.
speaker: narrator

text: The plaque under the Moose says: "Courtesy of Sierra Online Prop Dept."
speaker: narrator

text: You never saw a purple Saurus before you came to Spielburg, but it looks like a really stupid monster.  The plaque reads:  "Saurus slain by Hans Halfwitten".
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like it must have been a particularly nasty Troll.  You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark forest.  The plaque reads:  "Troll slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: This crossbreed of eagle and lion could have torn a man apart when it was alive.  The plaque reads: "Gryphon slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: Even in death, this monster remains awesome.  The plaque reads: "Dragon slain by Baron Stefan von Spielburg".
speaker: narrator

text: The head is like a panther's, but with a strong human-like quality.  It is still rather frightening.  The plaque reads:  "Cheetaur slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: This is certainly a weird one! You've never seen anything quite like it.  The plaque reads: "Antwerp slain by Two Guys From Andromeda".
speaker: narrator

text: This Adventurer's Guild Hall reminds you of the one in your home town.  The traditional Moose head and other stuffed monsters (Saurus, Troll, Gryphon, Dragon, Cheetaur, and the terrible Antwerp) adorn the walls.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the registration book on the desk and the bulletin board full of job listings.  The man seated by the fire must be the Guild Master.  He is snoring.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nothing here that you need, and the Guild Master may be more aware than he seems.
speaker: narrator

text: The desk drawers are empty.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no way to close the desk.
speaker: narrator

text: On the desk are an old but ornate leather-bound book and a quill pen in the ink bottle.
speaker: narrator

text: You see an ordinary pen and ink.
speaker: narrator

text: On the walls hang dusty, moth-eaten monster heads.  You recognize the purple Saurus, the Troll, the Gryphon, and the Dragon.
speaker: narrator

text: You figure that the black head must belong to the Cheetaur, and you have no idea what the four-eyed creature might be.  You are also not quite sure why the Moose head is considered traditional, but every Guild Hall seems to have one.
speaker: narrator

text: The desk holds a pen, an inkwell, and the Adventurer's Guild Registration and Log Book.
speaker: narrator

text: But you've already done that!
speaker: narrator

text: You sign your name into the Adventurer's Log Book with a flourish.
speaker: narrator

text: He already is.
speaker: narrator

text: "Oh, my young friend!  What shall we talk about?  I have so many stories.  Just ask me about something."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ja.  What with the baron losing his son and daughter, all the monsters, Baba Yaga, and now the brigands, it has been one thing after another."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Schultz and I have been friends for a long time.  He too has been a real adventurer.  Now, he is just the Sheriff of the town."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Even a thief can be a hero sometimes, as long as uses his skills only for good purposes.  But most thieves are far from being heroes."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "This is, of course, where an adventurer can find out where there is a need for someone brave and courageous.  There are jobs on the bulletin board over there."
speaker: wolfgang
